scenario: Nimbus SaaS reviews Camunda 8.8 event support before modeling a tenant lifecycle workflow that handles 18000 tenants per month. The architect considers reusing multiple events to bundle several trigger types into a single icon and checks the BPMN coverage page for support.
question: Which event family in the BPMN coverage page appears without any implementation marker on its icons in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Multiple and multiple parallel event icons across start, event subprocess, catch, boundary, throw, and end appear without an implementation marker on the events table
Escalation event icons across event subprocess, boundary, throw, and end variants appear without an implementation marker on the BPMN coverage events table
Error event icons across event subprocess, boundary, and end variants appear without an implementation marker on the BPMN coverage events table
Link event icons across catch and throw variants appear without an implementation marker on the BPMN coverage events table for execution in Camunda 8.8

a.text: Correct. The BPMN coverage events table renders every multiple and multiple-parallel event icon (start, event subprocess, catch, boundary, throw, end) without the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 does not execute them.

b.text: Incorrect. The escalation event icons in the events table (event subprocess, boundary, throw, end) all carry the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes escalation events.

c.text: Incorrect. The error event icons in the events table (event subprocess, boundary, end) all carry the implementation marker, indicating Camunda 8 executes error events.

d.text: Incorrect. The link catch and link throw event icons carry the implementation marker in the BPMN coverage events table, indicating Camunda 8 executes link events for jump-style flow.

explanation: Across the BPMN coverage events table, the multiple and multiple-parallel rows render every variant without the implementation marker, while escalation, error, and link events all appear with it.
